title: Pattern Composition
description: Single source of truth for named combinations of patterns. A composition is either a 'recipe' (abstract, normative design template) or a 'framework' (a real shipping software artefact — library, SDK, application, hosted product — that instantiates one or more recipes). Both share a members[] structure that lists pattern ids with a role; frameworks additionally carry identity (vendor, url, license, status), per-member evidence (verifiable upstream citations), a category, and may declare which recipes they instantiate. This supersedes the earlier recipes.schema.json and framework-coverage.schema.json. Pure data, no code.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier, unique across all compositions (recipes and frameworks share one id space, so a recipe id and a framework id must not collide).

aliases.description: Other names the composition has been called.

kind.description: 'recipe' = abstract design template (no upstream artefact; pattern members carry editor-judged necessity). 'framework' = real shipping software artefact (library, SDK, application, hosted product); pattern members must carry verifiable upstream evidence.

description.description: Two to four sentences. For recipes: the shape of system this composition produces and why those patterns belong. For frameworks: what the framework is, who builds it, and the shape of its agent loop.

intent.description: Optional one-sentence statement of what this composition accomplishes. Required on frameworks.

members.description: Patterns that participate in this composition. Pattern ids must resolve to entries in patterns-src/.

role.description: Allowed values depend on kind. For recipes: core | hardening | optional. For frameworks: core | first-class | supported | limited | via-integration | opt-in | anti-pattern | unknown. See $defs.role_recipe and $defs.role_framework.

note.description: Reader-facing prose: for recipes, why this pattern belongs to the shape; for frameworks, HOW the framework realises the pattern (which class, decorator, config, or workflow).

since.description: Frameworks only. First version or date this pattern became supported.

evidence.description: Frameworks only. Citations supporting the claim. Required on verified framework entries; may be empty on stubs.

evidence_status.description: Frameworks only. UI-facing flag summarising evidence completeness on THIS member. Glyph mapping for the visualiser: ✓ full (>=2 verifiable citations with quote+url), ◐ limited (exactly 1 citation, OR multiple citations but at least one lacks a quote), ○ none (evidence[] is empty). Derivable from evidence[] but stored explicitly so consumers don't need to re-implement the rule. Set by the Phase 2 verifier; do not author by hand.

category.description: Frameworks only. Sub-category within kind=framework. Determines which compositions-src/ shard this entry lives in.

vendor.description: Frameworks only. Organisation or person that builds or sells the framework.

language.description: Frameworks only. Implementation language(s), comma-separated. 'N/A' for hosted/closed frameworks.

successor.description: Frameworks only. If status=deprecated or rebranded, the composition id that replaced this one.

discontinued_date.description: Frameworks only. If status=discontinued, the ISO date the service was shut down.

first_released.description: Frameworks only. ISO date or year of first public release.

repo.description: Frameworks only. Source repository if open.

docs_url.description: Frameworks only. Canonical documentation entry point.

primary_use_cases.description: Frameworks only. Short noun phrases naming what people build with this.

agent_loop_shape.description: Frameworks only. One short paragraph naming the primary control-flow shape (e.g. 'graph of nodes with explicit state edges', 'imperative async event loop').

key_concepts.description: Frameworks only. Vendor-specific abstractions a user must learn.

pattern_composition.description: Optional Mermaid diagram source (without fences) showing how the patterns/concepts in this composition connect.

instantiates.description: Frameworks only. Recipe ids this framework instantiates. Lets a reader ask 'which frameworks realise the Modern Coding Agent shape?' and get an answer.

inherits_from.description: Other composition ids this composition specialises. Use to express parent-child relationships, e.g. a conversational-chatbot framework inherits_from a chatbot recipe. The relationship is declarative: child members[] must still list everything explicitly, but the link lets readers (and tooling) navigate up the specialisation chain. Each parent id must resolve to another entry in compositions-src/.

anti_patterns_avoided.description: Frameworks only. Catalog anti-patterns this framework explicitly designs against.

alternatives.description: Frameworks only. Other compositions a reader should compare with this one.

references.description: General references that document the composition as a whole (launch post, architecture talk, paper). Per-member citations live in members[].evidence.

last_analyzed.description: Frameworks only. ISO date this entry was last reviewed against the framework's current docs.

verification_status.description: Frameworks only. Honest grading of the entry. Glyph mapping for the visualiser: ✓ verified (every member carries a citation, all checked against upstream), ◐ partial (some members verified, some missing), ○ stub (structural skeleton, no per-member evidence yet), ⚠ needs-verification (was once populated but upstream has changed since last_analyzed). Pair with members[].evidence_status for per-pattern glyphs.

last_updated.description: ISO date this entry was last modified (any change to any field). Used to render 'X days ago' style timestamps in consumers. Bump whenever any non-trivial field changes. Independent of last_analyzed (verification timestamp) and of the verification-todo.json ledger.

status_in_practice.description: Confidence/maturity of the composition in real-world use. Glyph mapping for UI: ★★ mature (production-ready, settled), ★ emerging (stabilising, still evolving), · experimental (research-grade, may change), ✗ deprecated (superseded). Applies to both recipes and frameworks. Independent of verification_status (which grades evidence completeness, not real-world maturity).

role_recipe.description: Recipe member roles. core = removing this pattern breaks the recipe shape; hardening = adds safety, robustness, or observability on top; optional = common variation or upgrade.

role_framework.description: Framework member roles. core = the framework is built around this pattern; first-class = top-level documented API; supported = works idiomatically but not a headline feature; limited = requires recipe or workaround; via-integration = only through a third-party plugin; opt-in = available but off by default; anti-pattern = the framework deliberately rejects this pattern; unknown = not yet analysed.

quote.description: Short verbatim excerpt (<= ~300 chars) from the cited source that supports the claim.
